description: Perform a hydrological analysis of the Muncie river reach to determine the storage-discharge relationship and the Muskingum routing parameter 'K' using the HEC-RAS unsteady flow simulation output (Muncie.p04.hdf). Calculate the Total Channel Storage in acre-feet for the entire reach at each time step using the formula $Volume = \sum (Area_i \times Length_i)$ and extract the Outflow Discharge in cfs at the most downstream cross-section. Estimate the slope K in hours via linear regression of Volume vs Outflow. Save the processing script as ~/Documents/hec_ras_results/calculate_storage.py. Save the time-series data to ~/Documents/hec_ras_results/storage_discharge_data.csv with columns Time_Step_Index, Outflow_cfs, and Total_Storage_acft. Generate a plot of Storage vs Outflow at ~/Documents/hec_ras_results/storage_loop.png and save the estimated K value to ~/Documents/hec_ras_results/muskingum_k.txt in the format "Estimated K: X.X hours".
